ground_truth.title: Beginner Gardening Guide 101
ground_truth.author: Gayla Trail
ground_truth.main_content:
My experience as a gardener is rooted in the city, and that is what you will find in the 16 years of archives on this site: articles and stories on growing in raised beds, community garden plots, containers, high up on a rooftop, as well as directly in the ground. On this page I've compiled several of the posts that address how to get started gardening, but there are more to be found in the archive.

Plan: Learn about garden planning and design including how to identify cold spots, growing edible plants on the front stoop, making affordable pathways, staking methods for containers and in-ground plants, pruning tomatoes, making fences from pruned branches, and recycling bin gardens.

All About Soil: Learn how to amend in-ground as well as soilless mix for container planting, understand mycorrhizae and soil health, choose organic soil mixes, make fantastic compost bins, vermicomposting for apartment dwellers, urban composting for small spaces, and mix your own basic potting soil and seed-starting mix.

Plant: Get started growing from seeds and seedlings, propagate herbs through divisions, cuttings, offsets and more, and learn specific planting and growing care for various plants and crops.

Grow: Irrigate well and often using DIY pop bottle drip irrigation systems, protect sensitive plants with pop bottle cloches, extend the growing season organically, use organic and homemade fertilizers, and manage pests and disease.

Seasonal Preparation: Prepare for spring with raised bed prep and early planting, plan mid and late season planting, winterize your garden, clean up container gardens, bring potted plants indoors for winter, help plants survive indoors, and build D.I.Y. lighting systems.

Go Further: Learn about binomial nomenclature and why it matters for gardeners.
